title: Homepage First Impression
persona: A first-time visitor who has never heard of PublicDataWatch
goal: Figure out what this product is, who it is for, and what the first useful action should be.
success_question: Would a first-time visitor understand the product promise and know what to do next within one minute?
constraints: You will not read every section carefully.
You are willing to inspect the homepage but not wander all over the site.

variants.label: skeptical
variants.goal_append: You are skeptical that this is real civic data and you want evidence that it is not empty AI marketing.
extra_constraints: You distrust grand claims without concrete proof.

title: Homepage Arrival From Social Post
persona: A LinkedIn visitor clicking in from a short brag post
goal: Decide whether the landing experience matches the expectation created by a social post and whether it quickly leads into a useful action.
success_question: Does the homepage convert curiosity from social into a clear next step without feeling like a bait-and-switch?
constraints: You came in cold and have limited patience.
You are scanning, not researching.

title: Crime Preview For A Supported Address
persona: A renter checking whether a block feels concerning before making a decision
goal: Use a supported address and decide whether the preview quickly answers what is happening near the address.
success_question: Does the flow make it obvious what crime is nearby and whether the preview is worth paying attention to?
constraints: Do not assume technical knowledge.
Use a known supported address when needed, such as 851 Broadway Everett MA 02149.

variants.label: hurried
variants.goal_append: You are in a hurry and will abandon quickly if the preview feels slow or confusing.
extra_constraints: You will not wait long without visible progress.

title: Unsupported Address Experience
persona: A user in an uncovered area who still wants to know whether this site could help later
goal: Try an unsupported address and judge whether the unsupported state is respectful, clear, and worth engaging with.
success_question: Does the unsupported flow preserve trust and make the notify-me offer feel legitimate?
constraints: Use an address outside current coverage.
You are annoyed but still open to being notified later.

title: Use My Location Flow
persona: A mobile user who wants the app to just tell them about the place they are standing
goal: Evaluate whether the geolocation flow feels trustworthy, understandable, and effective.
success_question: Would a cautious user be willing to use location sharing here, and if they do, does the result feel correct?
constraints: You are privacy-sensitive.
Pay attention to consent cues and whether the resulting address feels believable.

variants.label: privacy-sensitive
variants.goal_append: You are especially uncomfortable granting browser location access unless the value proposition and privacy posture are obvious.
extra_constraints: You may refuse geolocation if the request feels premature.

title: Score Comprehension In Preview
persona: A normal person trying to make sense of whether the score is good, bad, or typical
goal: Use a supported address and judge whether the score explanation is understandable without technical knowledge.
success_question: Would a non-technical visitor understand what the score means and why they should care?
constraints: Avoid specialist crime-analysis language.
Prefer plain-language interpretation over technical explanation.

title: Trend Context Comprehension
persona: A cautious homebuyer looking for directional context, not raw statistical theory
goal: Judge whether the trend context feels useful or too technical for an ordinary decision-maker.
success_question: Does the trend explanation actually help a normal person interpret the address, or does it feel like analyst jargon?
constraints: You care about practical meaning, not methodology details.

title: Pricing Plan Comprehension
persona: A budget-conscious resident deciding whether the basic paid plan is worth it
goal: Understand the difference between the plans and decide whether the value ladder makes sense.
success_question: Can a normal person tell what $5 gets them, what $15 adds, and why either plan might be worth paying for?
constraints: You do not want to decode a feature spreadsheet.
You are price-sensitive.

variants.label: professional-buyer
variants.goal_append: You are a reporter or agent deciding whether the more expensive plan is justified for repeated work.
extra_constraints: You compare the pro plan against real recurring work needs.

title: Boston City Page Value
persona: A Boston-area user wanting to know whether the city page has a clear purpose
goal: Decide whether the Boston landing page stands on its own as a helpful region entry point.
success_question: Would someone landing directly on the Boston page understand what they can do next and why it matters?
constraints: Assume the user landed from search, not from the homepage.

title: Everett City Page Relevance
persona: An Everett resident checking whether this site seems local and credible
goal: Judge whether the Everett page feels specifically useful for Everett rather than like a generic city template.
success_question: Does the page feel local enough to build trust and hand users into the right next step?
constraints: You are sensitive to generic or templated local content.

title: New York Data-Type Expectation
persona: A New York visitor trying to understand what kind of data is actually available
goal: Determine whether the city page sets expectations honestly when the coverage is not crime-first.
success_question: Would a New York user understand what is available here without feeling misled?
constraints: Pay attention to whether the page implies unsupported features.

title: Explore Tool Discoverability
persona: A curious visitor who wants more than one address and wonders what the advanced tools are for
goal: Figure out whether the advanced map feels approachable and connected to the main value proposition.
success_question: Does the full data map feel like a sensible next step, or like a disconnected power-user tool?
constraints: You are not a GIS expert.
You may abandon if the page feels overwhelming.

title: Trend Reports Value
persona: A reporter trying to decide whether the trends surface is useful enough to revisit
goal: Decide whether the trends page communicates why its findings matter and who should care.
success_question: Would a reporter understand what the trends page offers and whether it is worth deeper exploration?
constraints: You care about useful signal, not decorative metrics.

title: Neighborhood Scores Orientation
persona: A user who clicked into scores but does not understand H3 or analytic scoring systems
goal: Judge whether the scores area provides enough context for a non-expert to know what they are looking at.
success_question: Does the page orient newcomers, or does it assume too much technical context?
constraints: You do not know what H3 means.
You are willing to scan but not study documentation first.

title: Help Center Utility
persona: A confused user looking for fast reassurance about how the product works
goal: Determine whether the help center actually reduces confusion or just repeats marketing language.
success_question: Would a confused user leave the help center more oriented and more trusting?
constraints: You came here because the product already confused you.

title: For Users Page Plain Language
persona: A resident trying to understand the product in ordinary language
goal: Evaluate whether the For Users page uses language that ordinary people can understand and trust.
success_question: Does the page explain the product in human terms instead of platform jargon?
constraints: You dislike buzzwords and over-explanation.

title: About Page Trustworthiness
persona: A skeptical visitor deciding whether this is a serious product or a throwaway side project
goal: Judge whether the About page builds confidence in the company and product.
success_question: Does the About page make the organization feel real, capable, and worth trusting?
constraints: You are looking for signs of seriousness, not fluff.

title: Free Preview And Trial Believability
persona: A user who expects a catch and assumes the preview/trial might be manipulative
goal: Decide whether the free preview and follow-on offer feel fair and believable rather than manipulative.
success_question: Would a skeptical person feel okay trying the free preview and considering the paid continuation?
constraints: You are sensitive to dark-pattern trial language.

variants.label: price-sensitive
variants.goal_append: You assume most subscriptions are not worth it and want a very concrete reason to pay.
extra_constraints: You compare the cost against doing nothing.

title: Unsupported User Starting From A City Page
persona: A visitor landing on the site hoping their city is covered
goal: Try to determine whether your city is covered and whether the site helps you recover if it is not.
success_question: Is coverage discoverable enough to avoid a bad surprise later in the funnel?
constraints: You are not willing to waste time entering addresses if coverage is unclear.

title: Mobile Navigation Clarity
persona: A mobile user trying to orient quickly without hunting through menus
goal: Determine whether mobile navigation makes the main surfaces discoverable without confusion.
success_question: Can a mobile user quickly find preview, cities, pricing, and help without getting lost?
constraints: Assume thumb-driven one-handed use.

title: Address Search Confidence
persona: A user nervous about whether the search box will understand their address
goal: Judge whether the homepage search affordance feels trustworthy and obvious enough to try.
success_question: Does the search box look like something that will work for a real address right away?
constraints: You are hesitant to invest effort unless the search seems credible.

title: Reporter Multi-Neighborhood Workflow
persona: A local reporter comparing multiple places for a story lead
goal: Assess whether the site makes a credible case for repeated professional use across more than one location.
success_question: Would a reporter believe this tool can support repeated neighborhood comparisons and not just one-off curiosity?
constraints: You need to see a path from first preview to deeper professional tooling.

variants.label: deadline-pressure
variants.goal_append: You are on deadline and need the value proposition to reveal itself quickly.
extra_constraints: You will not read long explanations before deciding.
